title: Xtra Flaky Polish Pork Pilaf
description: A spicy and flaky Polish dish featuring muscovado sugar, cooked in an agave leaf vessel with a papillote method, this recipe is dash-friendly and perfect for a weeknight dinner. The combination of pork, muscovado sugar, and spices creates a unique flavor profile.

ingredients.name: pork shoulder
ingredients.notes: boneless and cut into small pieces

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together muscovado sugar, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the pork pieces to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Cut a large agave leaf into a square shape and place it on a baking sheet.
Spoon the pork mixture onto the center of the agave leaf, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Drizzle the agave nectar over the pork and pour in the chicken broth.
Fold the agave leaf over the pork to create a papillote, and secure with kitchen twine.
Bake for 25 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 325 degrees Fahrenheit and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove the pilaf from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
